description:
To properly set the owner of /usr/bin/lastlog, run the command: 
$ sudo chown root /usr/bin/lastlog
rationale:
Unauthorized disclosure of the contents of the /var/log/lastlog file can reveal system data to
attackers, thus compromising its confidentiality.
ocil_clause: /usr/bin/lastlog does not have an owner of root
ocil:
To check the ownership of /usr/bin/lastlog, run the command: 
$ ls -lL /usr/bin/lastlog
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following owner: root
fixtext:
Change the owner of the directory /var/log/lastlog to root by running the following command:
$ sudo chown root /var/log/lastlog
srg_requirement: The Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 /usr/bin/lastlog directory must be owned by root.
title: Verify Owner on lastlog Command
